garding 	The 	2010 	Info-SUMMIT^'"
W h a t 	i s 	B I G 	L E A G U E 	M O N E Y ?

Why is it so readily available from Info-Marketing done the
Glazer-Kennedy way but, frankly, elusive in many other
businesses?....and how will THIS Info-SUMMIT*“, more so
than any other, call you up from ‘the minors’ and put Big
League Money at your fingertips?
The fee required for the Info-SUMMIT®“, if invested
in most financial options today, will yield about $100.00 	”
ayear. Goody. By attending, you could walk away with
everything you need to add at least $100,000.00 ayear to your
income. Which seems the better choice? Yes, I’m suggesting
info-marketing as insurance against present and predicted
financial storms almost sure to adversely affect any other
business, diminish income from any other source, reduce value
of investments of any other kind. And Ican promise that what
we’ve assembled for you at this Info-SUMMIT®'^' will make the
foundation of any info-biz stronger, empower you to make it
more profitable, or ease and speed your start of avaluable one.
These are the questions I’ll answer here! BIG LEAGUE
MONEY is mid to high 6-figures, 7-figures or even 8-figures
income. You may not aspire to the kind of money that gets you
featured on our friend Joan Rivers’ ‘How’d You Get So Rich? ’
television show. But I’m sure you want income, income stability
and wealth enough to live totally free of money worries... .to
enjoy two homes (or more), luxury vacations, your personal
interests and passions....to generously support charities and
causes... to live aBig League Life. That’s what I’m talking
‘ b o u t ! ! !
If you are NOT yet There, well, if not Now, then
When? Procrastination is the #1 habit of the poor, fear
and timidity their language, waiting and hoping for things to
somehow change for them, for better times and timing, their
mental illness. As you know, Ispend an awful lot of my time
answering that ‘how’d YOU get so rich?’ question -often from
people shocked by it, given my utter absence of credentials and
qualifications, or skeptical -believing that Ihave merely been
lucky or have some strange genetic advantage. While there is
no simple answer to the complex story of anybody’s success,
Iwill give you one of the biggest, most important answers
to the riddle of mine, right here, right now, entirely free of
charge: no waiting. Ican’t recall EVER waiting.
Last year, the average small to mid-sized business owner
made 25% to 50% less than the prior year, and most made less
in 08 than in 07. Many perished. That’s why it should have
been no surprise to Washington D.C. that the government’s
deficit in April 2010 alone did not stay under the $30-billion
projected, but topped $80-billion....gee, income tax receipts
were way, way, way down! But, while all these headwinds were
brutalizing many in most fields, my informal but extensive
‘survey’ of Info-Marketers told me most suffered income drops
of only about 10%, many stayed even, some gained abit, and
some skyrocketed upward.
When I’d had my fill of brutal winters, Imade adecision to
move to Phoenix on aThursday and left the next Tuesday.
When Idecided to sell acompany 1was running, Ipicked up
the phone, called the most likely buyer, arranged ameeting,
got on aplane and made the sale the next day (beginning a
relationship with Guthy-Renker, incidentally, that has benefited
me for more than 25 years). When my first book proposal was
rejected by all the agents Isent it to, Iflew to the American
Booksellers Convention, walked the show, accosted publishing
company executives in their booths, and got adeal. Icould go
on almost endlessly, certainly for days, with one anecdote after
another, making the same point. Inever waited when Ididn’t
have the money or didn’t have the time or didn’t know what
the hell Iwas doing or didn’t have any point of entry or for any
o t h e r r e a s o n .
There are many reasons for this, including the flexibility
inherent in Info-Marketing, permitting fast, agile change and
adaptation. But the bottom-line is this: Info-Marketing is just
about the only industry that can provide relative safety in the
worst of times and can create great riches at rapid pace in the
best of times. This is doubly, doubly, doubly true when your
info-biz is engineered the Glazer-Kennedy Way, with our
master-plans, based on our vast and successful experience, and
our unmatched access to true insider info from the biggest, best,
fastest growing and most profitable info-businesses in existence.
L - J f 	● ’
!
7 So let's go back to that Big League Life thing for
a m i n u t e . . .
-s’
If you are already There, then you should act
to safeguard it. Ican’t think of abetter way to do so than
by strengthening your present info-business or by adding an
info-business alongside your other business, as so many do.
I’m often asked these days: where should Iinvest my money for
safety and profit? Ianswer: damned if Iknow. But Ido know.
The best answer is in lists, relationship with lists, knowledge,
media savvy, and everything that combines to create and
support agreat info-marketing business.
The opposite behavior is evident in virtually all frustrated,
unhappy, unfulfilled, under-earning, under-achieving folks.
You choose your place -Big Leagues or not -by your choice of
those behaviors. Ideeply, fervently believe it.

CAL RIPKEN, 	JR.	I
O N 	T H E 	F I E L D :
During his 21 years with the Baltimore
Orioles, Ripken entered the record books 	R I P K E N 	W A Y
repeatedly: one of just eight to ever hit
400 home mns, and 3,000 hits. Broke Lou	i
Gehrig’s “iron man” record for consecutive
games played (2,130). Appropriately, Cal
is in the Baseball Hall of Fame.
O F F 	T H E 	F I E L D : P L A Y 	B A S E B A L L
Over 700,000 youths play Cal Ripken Baseball worldwide. He is adeveloper of
baseball-related businesses, including his own Ripken Youth Baseball Academy, with
youth-sized fields modeled after several big leagueljallparks; aone-of-a-kind baseball
complex featuring a6,000-seat stadium, in his home town, for aminor league team.
Early on, while still playing, he argued with his agents against merely getting typical
product/commercial endorsement deals in favor of more in-depth, proactive business
T H E R I P K E N W A Y
projects, aposition he has adhered to throughout his career.
A U T H O R 	& P U B L I S H I N G :
For parents. Cal developed aunique book on “Parenting Young Athletes The Ripken
Way for kids, “Play Baseball The Ripken Way and for all of us “Get In The Game:
8Elements of Perseverance That Make The Difference. ”He is continuing to develop

In afast-changing media environment, with an online environment where the rules-of-engagement are so fluid, it is easy to be seduced
^by shiny objects absent sound strategy for using them; distracted from the fundamental factors governing the attraction, ascension

Discover The FASTEST, EASIEST Way To Increase Your Profits Without Increasing Your Expenses...
Free Live Video Training Reveals The REAL, No B.S. Secret System For

Think You Hate Selling or Aren't Good At It
a
f t
If you are adoctor, lawyer, financial advisor, coach, consultant, restaurant owner or entrepreneur of ANY KIND, your financial success hinges on two factors:
1) Your ability to attract quality prospects. (That's marketing.) 2) Your ability to turn those prospects into customers, clients and patients. (That's SELLING.)
Odds are you probably love the first but not the second. But the cold hard truth is, unless you know how to close sales, you will ALWAYS struggle. In fact,
RIGHT NOW you are LOSING MONEY because you or your sales teams are not closing as many sales as they could. Here's another sobering thought: Because
your closing skills are NOT where they should be, agood portion of the hard earned money you spend on marketing is being WASTED. MORE BAD NEWS: Even
if you're smart enough to go buy some books to improve your sales skills, odds are what you'll learn is outdated, outmoded and DOES NOT WORK with today's
sophisticated customers, clients, and patients. In fact, using these old-fashioned closing techniques is HURTING you and costing you money!
But now you have the opportunity to learn the System that’ll explode your sales like afireworks display on the Fourth of July
Join Dan Kennedy, Dave Dee and GKIC Sales Manager Extraordinaire, Nick Loise for 4hours of LIVE ONLINE VIDEO training session where they'll
reveal the bullet-proof System for closing more sales. Discover the powerful 5step sales SYSTEM, how to handle the 7different kinds of objections...making you
and your team practically IMPERW0US to the word "NO;" the SECRET "Buying Signals" aprospect gives off that should be your cue to close and much more.
IMPORTANT: This is aREAL training -not asales pitch. The entire four hour broadcast will be turned into aproduct and sold after this one-time only live event

I N S I D E R ’ S C I R C L E
D e a r - To d d , ,
It’s downright criminal-when you spend
*gooa~rtioney to create fails to deliver the
results you desire it’s like being robbed at
g u n p o i n t !
Here is aGREAT list of tips
on writing copy from one of
GKIC’s certified experts: John
C a r l t o n . T h e b o l d n u m e r i c
points are John’s (I’ll kibbutz
alongside each).
1. Use POWER words. (Power
w o r d s l i k e “ a s s a u l t ” a n d
“humiliate” imply action. Force.
Movement. Confidence. Use
them whenever you can.)
2. Use Action verbs, not
Adjectives. (Write in Active
voice “someone did something”
not “something was done” -and
avoid flowery adjectives. Write
direct and to the point.
3. Use Storytelling Verbs...
imagine... dream... (People
LOVE stories! Find ways to
weave tale-telling into copy.)
4. Apply the So-What Test.
After each sentence think, “So
what?” (This FORCES you
to get into the mind of your
customer and their objections
before you write.)
5. Get the rhythm of the copy
by reading it out loud. (Strive
to write like you speak-make it
sound like you, not atextbook.)
6.it-it’s easy, it’s fast, it’ll
change your life. (Your copy
must flow smoothly like aleaf
being carried downstream.)
7. Study great copywriting.
(Save all direct mail pieces you
get. And take advantages of
GKIC’s top notch programs like
Copy Confidential!)
Don’t get taken for aride. You now have
the chance to gain inside access to the
m a r k e t i n g a n d c o p y i d e a s , t a c t i c s , a n d
s t r a t e g i e s u s e d t o g u e l G K I C ’ s 8 - F i g u r e -
and-growing success, “Copy Confiden¬
tial” will give you the ammo you need to
create marketing campaigns that’ll bring
in cash by the truckload,
2>a/-dy
GKIC Director Of Marketing
PS: We only have alimited number of
seats available-so you need to act now.

-- 143 of 830 --

Immortality and Desire For Meaning. (Ref., Man’s
Search For Meaning by Victor Frankel.)
LEVEL 2; Deeper, to sell more complex propositions
and/or costly goods or services, you can utilize the
relatively simple, formulaic approaches to psychology
baked into copy, like my list of “E-FACTORS. This is,
essentially, Zig’s “Emotional Logic In Selling”. It is
MEDIUM-Impact Copy. It reached the prospect on an
emotional level, co-mingled thought and feeling, and
motivated apurchase more than it made asale.
NOTE: The following is just aTASTE of what
I'll reveal in detail during my "Seven Sorceries
of Mind Controi Copy" sessions at the Info-
S U M M I T
literally 	aseminar-in-a-seminar!
Interestingly, the deeper the meaning, the more hidden
the desire, the more powerful it is if hit upon, and the
more price elasticity there is in the sale. An item you
struggle to sell for $50 can be successfully sold for
$500, if it can be attached to powerful hidden desires.
S M a f u l l 3 > 2 h o u r s o f c o n t e n t . . .
1 1
T h e 	I n s a n e l y 	P o w e r f u l 	C o p y
S e c r e t 	o f 	Ta p p i n g 	I n t o 	t h e
H I D D E N 	D e s i r e . . .
LEVEL 3: Deeper still, to persuade at more
sophisticated level, to have more impact, we get to the
psychology that I’m talking about here, and laying out
in my Info-SUMMIT®”^ Session. This is HIGH-Imnact
Copy, that makes aperson stop, think, ponder; makes a
person feel and engage with the idea and the person
advancing the idea. Think of the movie that provoked
conversations you are having days later vs. the movie
you recall only the great car chase or best ribald joke
f r o m .
How do you come to understand the hidden desire(s) of
the people you seek to attract, interest and sell to? And
how do you translate that into copy, overtly as Steve
did, or more subtly? When you communicate, to
persuade, influence or motivate, you want to have
IMPACT. You want to make people think, feel, agree
with you, buy into your ideas. You want it to be hard
for them to quickly and easily dismiss your message or
proposition.
/ /
People usually buy things they know they need or
want, which often turns those products and services
into commodities with thin profits. People are sold
things they never knew existed or never thought they
needed or wanted -and that’s where copy that taps
into hidden desire enters the picture.
here are Three Levels of Impact
A t t a i n a b l e 	w i t h 	C o p y 	^
People want things they don’t know they want, can’t
enunciate, are uncomfortable and secretive about, keep
private, keep suppressed in their subconscious. These
hidden desires have incredible power.
Level 3is the fun stuff, the taking control of a
person’s thoughts and having him not just buy
something, but bond with it and you, and feel
“elevated” by the purchase.
LEVEL 1: It is often possible to sell things simply
with good mechanics, tried ‘n true copy structure, and
competent writing. This is definitely true at the
commodity level. In the info-business, two short
paragraphs of such copy in acatalog were all that was
needed to sell over 25,000 copies of a2-cassette, $39
product Ionce published, "Effective Telephone
Techniques for Receptionists and Secretaries." This is,
however, LOW-Impact Copy. Here today to make a
simple sale, forgotten tomorrow.
i i i u i	
1iiiI‘ 	: 	i i
Some years back, Steve Harrison wrote aheadline and
letter, aimed at book authors and would-be authors, that
ignored the usual, superficial motivating factors -get
on Oprah, be abestselling-list author, promote yourself
and your business, etc. -with the number of people
who would mourn your death, travel from distant
places to your funeral, think and talk about how you
influenced their lives long after your departure. It
performed magnificently. It leveraged Desire For ,
If you are interested in operating at ahigher level of
influence in info-marketing, this little preview should
be more than enough to convince you of the value of
being at my Info-SUMMIT®^ Session on “The Seven
Secret Sorceries of Mind Control Copy”
Sign up NOW at www.dankennedv.com/infosu mmit

Dear GKIC Member,
Pooh Bear never ventured from his little woods. The Hobbit, Bilbo Baggins says H^doesn’t like
adventures because they make one late for dinner. Iimagine this is the way alot of people
secretly feel about exposure to new and big opportunities and exposure to people with no
particular advantage over them achieving agreat deal more, to from-scratch millionaire and
multi-millionaires, to 7-figure income earners. It’s unsettling.
I’m confident that’s what is really behind many peoples’ decision NOT to attend the Info-
SUMMIT^'^: the prospect of being exposed to and confronted by areality of extraordinary
opportunity and hundreds of people successfully creating business lives of their preferences
threatens to make one very, very uncomfortable. More so, the prospect of actually making
dramatic changes in one’s business life, changing or adding abusiness, managing new media,
putting self out there as an author or guru or leader intimidates. So many who might be liberated
beyond imagination by what they would discover, instead talk themselves into simply staying
home. Staying in the little woods they know so well rather than venturing to even look beyond
t h e h o r i z o n .
I’m not saying that’s YOU. Obviously, Idon’t know. Isuppose there are lots of other reasons to
pass up the Info-SUMMIT^”^. But Ithink it’s worth confronting your motives.
Consider four facts. One, the U.S. and global economies are troubled -so much so that best-
run companies like FedEx have recently downgraded their 2013 earnings forecasts and warned
of adeepening recession; or so much so that Bemanke has announced the printing of $40-billion
amonth to be pumped into the markets as “support”, for an indeterminable number of months.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

(
i
Fakery by the Fed to prop up aweak, wounded and ailing economy. Countless kinds of ‘regular’
businesses are threatened by this poor economy, by downward price pressure, destruction of
profits, Obamacare, new taxes, new regulations. This suggests wisdom in search for new or
different or better or less threatened opportrmity -areason to join us at the Summit. If you have
aleaky old boat that isn’t awhole lot of fun to pilot anjmiore and may not be up to the coming
storms, heck, don’t skip the Boat Show! Two, there is awave of change affecting the info¬
marketing industry, from demographics of its consumers to business models to product
and price to media and technology. Whether you’re asuccessful veteran with athriving info-
business, anewcomer, or avirgin, keeping pace with tomorrow is amandate for today, and
there’s no other clearinghouse and exchange of cutting edge information than this Summit.
Three, this is THE once-a-year gathering of the leaders of this field and the vendors they rely on
for implementation, the fraternity of info-marketers, where you can meet and hear from and pick
the brains of the best. Four, more than l/5‘^ of the people in attendance will be at their 4* to 10
Summit, will have entered info-marketing by my leadership, will own or have come from music
schools and rug cleaning businesses and insurance agencies and every imaginable business and
created businesses in info-marketing paying them $250,000 to $500,000, $1-million, $3-
million ayear. They keep returning to this Summit. Do they know something you don’t? Quite
probably.
Frankly, the case for venturing from your comfort nest, to this very different
world of ours is compelling -especially in light of the incredible amount of danger and hazard
for businesspeople, and indight of the grand new^pportunities always produced"
be economic, social, industrial, technological and demographic upheaval. I’ve candidly
exhausted my making of this case, in the letters and literature already sent your way, so this is
probably the last you’ll hear from me on this subject. And if you are unsettled or intimidated by
big opportunities and bold ideas, it may be bestyow do stay home. If coming face to face with
hundreds and hundreds of ‘ordinary folks’ shattering limitations and rising to top 1%’er incomes,
creating wealth from scratch, and re-making their business lives to suit them, often at breakneck
speed, is depressing instead of inspiring, and makes you feel bad, well, maybe it is best that you
stay home. Ihave never suggested that fortune-making or info-marketing is for the faint of heart.
If it is your decision to stay home, Ihope you make it for some reason other than timidity
or passivity, some reason other than self-disqualification. The disqualifying of oneself from
opportunity is, Ithink, one of the saddest most common behaviors of the masses. That
comes, of course, from aguy with aprofound life experience bias: I’m utterly unqualified in any
traditional sense for everything Ido, for every space I’ve stuck my nose into and taken huge
sums of money out of, and to the distress of some, unabashed by it. Bruce Wayne did not go to
school and get degrees in Criminology and Criminal Psychology nor pass the police exam; he
got himself into shape, put on acostume and arrived as acrime-fighter. The power of self¬
appointment and self-definition and self-determination, which is at the heart of info-marketing.
Anyway, there remains room for you at the Summit, as Iwrite this. Should you stubbornly
ignore my urging and stay home, you miss alot....

Back in the 1800s enterprising entrepreneurs converged on Colorado
in search of gold. At that time the wide open west was frontier land
-undeveloped and naturally beautiful, but filled with danger. These
adventurous souls had to deal with treacherous mountains and terrain, wild
animals, harsh weather, ruthless competition and unfriendly natives.
They were taking abig chance. They may strike gold. But they could lose
everything, including their lives. Their entrepreneurial spirit and sense of
adventure drove them to push on and go against the grain.
Everyone will be looking to discover and conquer new online

ASPECIAL 	INVITATION 	FROM 	DAN 	KENNT^DY
FORINFORMATIONMARKETINGENTREPRENEURS,PUBEISHERS
SPEAKERS &PIATEORM SAFES PROS,
MARKETERS 	OF 	EVENTS 	AND
COACHING 	PROGRAMS,
A N D 	C O P Y W R I T E R S
U£/)WNS \}iJy Zl'jt -Ut,
to 'U4f^01^ 	'M)t -^jlA^AA^'yttcdt
In this letter, 1am going to TEACH and REVEALimportant things about selling in the
information marketing field, and I’ll wager in advance that at least one of them will be
something you have not considered before, and that you will want to know more about.
If Imwrong, if this is “old hat” to you, the letter itself and the time you invest in reading
it are GUARANTEED. You can write to me, tell me why you found this all uninteresting
and without value, and I’ll send you $25 or I’ll donate $100 to the charity of your
choice. In other words, IDARE YOU to go through this “lesson” and come out the
other end bored, disinterested and without new thought about improving your selling
and sales copy.
F r i e n d ,
Words converted to cash and customers. That is what copywriting is about, in abasic
and fundamental way. But you can learn and understand the mechanics and
mechanical process of copywriting, but still not produce exceptional results -
because imderneath the mechanics, behind the words, there is unique psychology.
For 40 years. Eve practiced that alchemy, to hundreds of millions of
dollars, in info-marketing, IN EVERY MEDIA -print advertisements (my ads for
self and clients have appeared in over 300 different magazines and trade
journals), direct-mail here and abroad, TV, radio, inside seminar rooms, on VHS,
DVD and audio cassette and CD "audio sales letters", in online video-assisted
and video sales letters, webinars, product launches, etc., etc., etc. and etc! There is
no application where my methods and my copy has not made millions of dollars.
It's important to know that the psychology behind all these applications is
the same. Buyers of success education, information, training, coaching and
opportunities are responsive because o/certain thought patterns they already
have in place, that are aroused, stimulated and directed to your proposition by
the copy you write.
This alone is acritical fact: we do not succeed in this field by presenting
brand new concepts to people who've never thought about them before. We
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 202 of 830 --

claim to do this, because it makes us heroic. But it is not how we profit. Instead,
connect with those who have certain thought patterns already in place.
Think of it as drilling for oil. You can’t drill ahole where there is no oil
already underneath the ground and somehow make adry hole turn into an
oil 	weU 	-no 	matter 	how 	clever, 	expert 	or 	determined 	you 	may 	he.
Cleverness, expertise and persistence fall when drilling where there is no
oil. The oU must already be there, found, then pumped.
When Iwrite copy in information-marketing, for whatever application -
speech or video or audio script, advertisement, sales letter -Iam NEVER
writing to appeal to the entire audience it will be put in front of. This is futile,
wasteful, and harmful to the selling objective. If you create too broad an appeal,
you fail to connect to the thought patterns of the very best prospects -which are
always asmall minority; asmall number of golden needles in ahaystack of sour
straw. The most profitable copy is magnetic to the gold needles.
w 	e
(Nearly 	Flunked 	f)lgebra.
But ICan Tell You How 2% =50%
Let's destroy ordinary math. There, 2% =2%, nothing more, nothing less.
Say otherwise, you flunk their tests. But where you and Ilive, where big money
is made, 2% may =50%, and it is vital you grasp why.
Let us assume we get a2% response from adirect-mail campaign, or from an audience
driven through aweb portal and avideo presentation (like cattle on acattle drive). Did
we get 2%? Yes. And no. Because the majority receiving the piece or viewing the video
are NOT eligible to buy because they lack the necessary, existent (active or latent)
thought patterns needed to buy. The real audience of real prospects may be 20% of the
whole, thus the 2% of the whole equals % of the eligible 20%.
Re-read that paragraph.
This is amath fact.
We can "manage" this fact two ways.
One, we can strive to improve the eligibility of ie. the selection of and
attraction of appropriate prospects likely to have the needed thought patterns in
which we invest our sales copy however it is delivered. Every smart marketer
does this. Rev. Rick Warren describes it brilliantly in his book on church
marketing. The Purpose-Drive Church, abook Ioften recommend. Italked about it
at the 2013 SuperConference, in my Whale-Hunt presentation.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 203 of 830 --

Two, we can “Py'" "™lch" With the existenlthousht
pttemsmostlikelytowork"forus,whatwesell,andthekindsofcustomers
we want so that we get 90% of the eligible 20%.
Thefirstisamarketingfunction,notthesubjectofthisletter,butsomethineI
work with clients on agreat deal.
The	second	is	acopy	strategy	and	copywriting	function.	Which	is	the	subject	of	this
letter.Matchingsalescopytotheexistentthoughtpatternsofthehighest
probability and most desired buyers is an art that uses science. It can even result
"C copywriter getting "A" results.
Over The River And Through The Woods.
To Where Do We Take The Prospect?
We actually did go over ariver and through awoods to get to
grandmother's house when Iwas akid. It was ajourney.
m 	a
Now, Another 	Lesson
From Dr. Seuss: Oh, the places you'll go....
To become abuyer through copy, aprospect has to be guided and be
taken on one or more journeys. This is why short copy is so often ineffective and
unsuccessful, because these are journeys, not short walks. It is through the
journey that the prospect's necessary thought patterns are stimulated and
directed, so that he becomes aware that the person selling to him and the thing being
sold to him match with his own thinking on the subjects involved.
Make yourself anote. That's aprofitable goal:
Writing copy so that your prospect becomes aware that the person seiiing to him and the
thing being soidto him match with his own thinking on the subjects invoived.
Assume we are selling to die-hard racists. It is crude and dangerous and
insufficient to merely put the Confederate flag on our letterhead and use the
hated N-word. They are racists because 0/certain deeper, more leveragable
thought patterns. For example, they likely have avery traditional, and very
conservative viewpoint and asuspicious, fearful world view. They likely believe
they are hurt and disadvantaged by government forced 'affirmative action', and
feel they deserve more credit than they get as great Americans. And so on. And
we never need agree with or even nod at their racism in order to tap into these
deeper thought patterns. We can structure ajourney or journeys within our copy
that take this particular reader, listener or viewer from aplace of simmering rage
and resentment, disaffection, fear, longing for asimpler time, etc. to adesire for
what we are all about and what we offer. Iam, not, of course, suggesting you
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 204 of 830 --

seek out racists as your customers. Iam using astark illustration of the broader
principle: beneath the superficial identifiers of any audience lies deeper, more
leveragable thought patterns.
So, here Iam going to briefly talk about one of the journeys. There are 5
that Tm going to present as part of my Extended Length, 3.5-Hour Session at the
2013 Info-SUMMIT on The Psychology Of Winning Copy for Info-Marketers. But as
example, here, weTl look briefly at one of the most familiar: The Hero's Journey.
' a
The title Napoleon Hill wound up with, THINK And Grow Rich, is a
worthy 4x6" card tacked where you see it often. It's easy to lose sight of the fact
that quality of thinking governs quality of income, that elevating the quality of
one's thinking is an essential element to elevating one's income to its highest
possible points.
OF COURSE.. ..I recognize the risk in what Tm doing here, introducing
sophisticated thinking about the sales copy you need, use, all too frequently slap
together hastily. You may react as if offered an art appreciation class, when you
really wanted asofa-sized painting or apaint-by-numbers kit. So, I
acknowledge, 	this 	session 	I'm 	preparing 	for 	the 	Info-SUMMIT 	is 	NOT 	going
to appeal to everybody. There is acertain level of "mental ambition" required.
I'd note, almost everybody has adesire for greater results and greater income.
Many have legitimate ambition for greater results and greater income. But fewer
have ambition to be more intelligent and sophisticated about how they get those
results and income. Some actually resist or even resent the proposition that
greater sophistication is aworthy objective; to them, it is seen as aggravating
complexity.
You, individually, Ican't know. Ihave always been fascinated by digging
for the deeper truths about whatever Tm involved in. Ididn't just read Collier on
copy, Iread Freud. It is apeeling away at the layers of the onion. The more layers
you peel in understanding, the greater your advantage in the marketplace. If yon
are the type of person who wants best advantage and is willing to deepen your
understanding, and deepen it some more, and again deepen it some more, then
you will find this Info-SUMMIT Session of mine profoundly fascinating.
The 	Copy 	Secret 	of The 	HIDDEN 	Desire
People usually buy things they know they need or want, which often turns
those products and services into commodities, with thin profits. People are sold
things they never knew existed or never thought they needed or wanted -and
that's where copy that tapes into hidden desire enters the picture.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 205 of 830 --

People want things they don't know they want, can’t enunciate, are uncomfortable
and secretive about, keep private, keep suppressed in their subconscious. These hidden
desires have incredible power.
Some years back, Steve Harrison wrote aheadline and letter, aimed at
book authors and would-be authors, that ignored the usual, superficial
motivating factors -get on Oprah, be abestselling-list author, promote yourself
and your business, etc. -with the number of people who would mourn your
death, travel from distant places to your funeral, think and talk about how you
influenced their lives long after your departure. It performed magnificently. It
leveraged Desire For Immortality and Desire For Meaning. (Ref., Man's Search For
Meaning by Victor Frankel.)
For trade journal ads, direct-mail and an online campaign aimed at high
income earners in the financial advisor profession, for abook linked to aseminar,
with my client Matt Zagula, we similarly set aside all the usual promises being
made by all the other advertisers -make more money, sell bigger cases, fill up
your free workshops, etc. -and focused instead on the daring assertion that
everything they thought they knew about selling was dead wrong, and further,
we focused on the idea of amore sophisticated approach, actually selling trust
(not products, services or financial solutions). This was hugely successful
because it connected with ahidden desire of many high income earners: the
desire to be distinctively different, smarter and more sophisticated. Iwrote this
copy to the person already making ahigh 6-figure to 7-figure income, bored with
the way he was earning it. Here, the chief hidden desire is To Be Keenly Interested
(Un-Bored) With Work.
Interestingly, the deeper the meaning, the more hidden the desire, the
more powerful it is if hit upon, and the more price elasticity there is in the sale.
How do you come to understand the hidden desire(s) of the people you
seek to attract, interest and sell to? And how do you translate that into copy,
overtly as Steve did, or more subtly?
Ah, grasshopper....in my Info-SUMMIT Session, we'll talk about the
"lifeline theory of hidden desires", apsychology-based means of building a
profile of your prospect.
The Three levels ef IMPACT
Hopefully, when you communicate, to persuade, influence or motivate,
you want to have IMPACT. You want to make people think, feel, agree with you,
buy into your ideas. You want it to be hard for them to quickly and easily
dismiss your message or proposition.
There are three levels of impact attainable with copy:
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 206 of 830 --

LEVEL1:Itisoftenpossibletosellthingssimplywithgoodmechanics,triedntruecopy
andcompetentwriting.Thisisdefinitelytrueatthecommoditylevel.Nothing
more is needed for aVal-Pak coupon about pizza or, for that matter, replacement
windows. In the iirfo-business, two short paragraphs of such copy in acatalog were all
that was needed to sell over 25,000 copies of a2-cassette, $39 product Ionce published,
'Effective Telephone Techniques for Receptionists and Secretaries.'
LOW-Impact Copy. Here today to make asimple sale, forgotten tomorrow.
LEVEL 2: Deeper, to sell more complex propositions and/or costly goods or services, you
utilize the relative simple, formulaic approaches to psychology baked into copy, like
my list of "E-FACTORS." This is, essentially, Zig's "Emotional Logic In Selling". This is
MEDIUM-Impact Copy. It reached the prospect on an emotional level, co-mingled
thought and feeling, and motivated apurchase more than it made asale.
LEVEL 3: Deeper still, to persuade at more sophisticated level, to have more impact, we
get to the psychology that I'm talking about here, and laying out in my Info-SUMMIT
Session. This is HIGH-Impact Copy, that makes aperson stop, think, ponder; makes a
person feel and engage with the idea and the person advancing the idea. Think the
movie that provoked conversations you are having days later vs. the movie you recall
only the great car chase or best ribald joke from.
s t r u c t u r e .
This is, however.
c a n
Another way to think about this is that Level 1is structured selling.
Attention-Interest-Desire-Action. Problem-Agitate Problem-lnvalidate Options-The
Solution. Value v. Price =Irresistible Bargain. And so on. Level 2is raising a
person's fascination and getting them to see personal relevance, then to motivate
themselves to buy. Level 3is the fun stuff, the taking control of aperson's
thoughts and having him not just buy something, but bond with it and you, and
feel "elevated" by the purchase.
If you are interested in operating at ahigher level of influence in info¬
marketing, this little preview should be more than enough to convince you of the
value of being at my Info-SUMMIT Session on The Psychology of Winning Copy.

Who 	has 	used
INFLUENTIAL Writing to
Great Purpose and Benefit?
At atime he was under attack as simple
pomographer, Hugh Hefner wrote aseries
of articles defining and describing The
Playboy Philosophy’, which incorporated
libertarian and constitutionalist politics,
gender equality and feminism, aspirational
lifestyle, and other matters quite
disconnected from “pictures of naked
women" -and he succeeded at making
Playboy about something more important
than those pictures or ahedonist’s magazine,
and, to considerable extent, silencing critics.
Actress Suzanne Somers has used articles
and books on her controversial positions on
cancer prevention and treatment, causes of
disease, womens’ health issues notably
including hormone therapy, etc. to establish
herself as acelebrated authority on
alternative health, as well as supporting a
multi-faceted business enterprise in that field
-despite having no formal training or
academic credentials in the field .
David Ogiivy was the first to set himself
apart from, and place himself on ahigher
plane than his competitors in the advertising
field, by writing abook about his philosophy
of advertising. He built what was in its time
the largest and most prominent ad agency in
the world.
Martha Stewart has used her own
magazine, books, blog and other media to
champion an approach to living, lifestyle, and
home, it the basis for agiant commercial
enterprise incorporating licensing of her
brand to countless products and joint-
ventures with Macy’s, Home Depot, and
others.
And, just in case you think this doesn't apply
to you and your ‘ordinary’ business, consider
that much of her business has to do with
selling towels and toilet seat covers, dishes
and kitchenware, and home furnishings.
These people used/use INFLUENTIAL
Writing to make themselves more important
and significant to people than ‘just another
merchant’; to connect with people as
advocates of philosophies; to influence
thinking, not just make sales.

intrigue, even obsession with communication, persuasion,

Writing to Influence Thinking,
for Strategic Purpose
The week Iwas completing this letter, my friend
and speaking peer Jim Rohn passed away. Tens
of thousands of people -me included -have
been beneficially influenced by Jim's written
works and recordings.
His plain-spoken wisdom about personal
development, initiative, discipline, persistence
and work are universally meaningful, and can be
read, listened to, and enjoyed at face value.
However, if you understand their original
strategic purposes, you can see them in an
entirely different light, and appreciate their
construction in adifferent way.
Jim spent years almost entirely working within
the multi-level marketing/network marketing, as a
speaker, trainer and author, so his parables,
stories, lessons and material was all designed to
influence the thinking, attitudes and behavior of
the distributors and sales agents in ways the
parent companies needed them influenced,
notably to discourage easy quitting, encourage
patience and persistence, immunize against
others’ negative criticism.
So, for example, Jim's messages about “planting
and -later- han/esting" have double meaning;
the apparent, on-surface, universal meanings,
but also the underneath, specifically directed
message to MLM distributors about recruiting. Of
course, you have to have experiential
understanding of the MLM business to even
recognize the coded, purposeful messages
'hidden' in the bigger, broader message.
This is an important aspect of INFLUENTIAL
Writing that Iexplore in my Program: the
construction and use of coded messages
You’re familiar with how dog whistles work: only
dogs hear them, people don't. Being
sophisticated enough to imbed "dog whistles"
into your writing, that only those you specifically
seek connection and influence with, is an
advanced, high-level strategy and skill you can
gain enormous benefit from having as your own.
/ /
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

August;	The good news is that these marketing efforts generate aflood of
prospects. The bad news is that apoor sales process prevents many
businesses &entrepreneurs from closing nearly as many sales as they
could or should. Profits are almost literally being flushed down the toilet.
Are YOU ready to make more money with less hassle using Ultra-
A D VA N C E D S a l e s & P e r s u a s i o n S t r a t e g i e s ?
As one of the world’s highest-paid copywriters. I’ve been paid
millions of dollars over the years to create direct marketing pieces that
attract prospects iike bees to honey. I’ve also been teaching direct
response marketing tactics to people for over 30 years. Anyone willing to
study direct response marketing and apply what they learn should be
attracting large numbers of prospects fairly quickly, but...that’s only half the battle.
C M n e x
Most people incorrectly put most of their focus on creating the next great direct response marketing piece. They
make the mistake of bringing prospects right up to the point to which the sale is made or is NOT made...and then they
drop the ball by not having awell-oiled sales process in place to close the deal.
Let me ask you 3quick questions.
1. Are you neglecting your sales process because you’re too focused on marketing?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 239 of 830 --

2. Can you see that improving your sales process can create aprofit
explosion?
3. Are you ready to make more money from your existing prospects?
But the problem does NOT stop there...It goes way beyond whether
or not you make the initial sale. The sales process impacts the relationship
with your customers from the point of sale forward...and that’s where you II
discover the real profit, value, money, and equity in any business. So if
your marketing &sales processes are not seamlessly integrated, it could
be costing you afortune.
None of this is your fauit. We don’t spend as much time on the sales
process because it’s not as much fun as creating the next great marketing
piece. It’s not as much fun as finding the next lumpy object to put in your
envelope or inventing creative ways to tie in the stuffed barking dog or
fuzzy dice to your marketing campaign. But while the sales process may
not be as fun as the marketing process, it is the “missinq link” to
generating more cash from your existing business.
Soifyou’renotmakingasmuchmoneyasyoucould,should,orwanttothenyou’llwanttopaycloseattention
because /know how to solve this problem once and for all. I’ve been doing it for my clients for decades but until now
I’ve held these “Ultra-ADVANCED Sales &Persuasion Strategies”close to the vest. Since I’m in semi-retirement Ifeel
comfortable sharing these powerful strategies with you now.
The first thing to understand is that people want to buy. People want to believe, they want to buy, and contrary to
popular beliefs, they have the ability to buy but at some point in the sales process they become UNsold. Why is this? It
might be something as silly as dirty fingernails...
Are there "Dirty Fingernails" in YOUR Sales Process?
The other day Ispoke to someone who told me astory about arecent experience shopping for amajor banquet.
It would have been in the neighborhood of a$25,000.00 purchase. She visited four different facilities, met with four chefs,
caterers, etc. and picked one. Iasked why she didn’t choose the others. Incredibly, the reason she did NOT choose one
of them was simply because the salesperson had dirt under two of her
fingernails and she couldn’t get it out of her mind. That’s it...
...a possible $25,000.00 sale lost due to dirty fingernails. How painful
would losing $25,000.00 in sales over something so seemingly trivial
be to your business?
It could have been some other simple thing like being afew
minutes late for an appointment, asalesman’s shoes not being shined, or
an unfriendly receptionist. If you’re not identifying and eliminating the “dirty
fingernails” in your sales process, you’re missing out on sales and you
probably don’t even know it.
The fact is that most businesses are using the same old-
fashioned sales techniques. Is YOUR business one of them? You may be using techniques you learned in books, in
school, at seminars, or by trial &error...but the business landscape continues to change rapidly &dramatically so your
prospects have grown increasingly immune to these conventional sales approaches.
Techniques that worked effectively yesterday get lost in the clutter of today’s overhyped sales
environment.. .that’s why most businesses are seeing their response rates drop every year. Don’t expect this trend to
change anytime soon.
Today’s prospects are wise to the world. They’ve seen all the dog &pony tricks used to sell them in the past and
they’ve learned how to recognize and avoid these old, worn out &ineffective approaches. Here’s something else for you

And Carnegie opened his pocket watch, held it face up, and waited.
Napoleon Hill said yes, with asecond to spare.
20 years later, the no-longer-young Napoleon Hill had his book,THINKAND GROW RICH, published,
summarizinghisinterviewsandinvestigationsintothelivesofover500oftheseextraordinaryachieversand
leaders.Hedidsupporthimselfthroughthe20years,asanoutstandingdirect-responseadcopywriter,lecturer,
salesman,andashecompletedthemanuscriptfortheactualencyclopedia'LAWSOFSUCCESS',condensed
as 'THINK AND GROW RICH', as aspeech writer for President Roosevelt. His book has lived since as the
most famous, most read book on the subject of success.And you may be thinking; I've read it. Iknow this.
Nothing new here. But be abit patient, for this is just the beginning....
11 elate this story to you, incidentally, based upon Hill's own writings, and conversations Ihad with two
men who knew him and worked with him personally, my friend, Foster Hibbard, and W. Clement Stone.
Mr.Stoneafterworkingonthe'ThinkAndGrowRich'televisioninfomercial,formyclient,theGuthy-Renker
Corporation, in conjunction with the Napoleon Hill Foundation.

I M I L L I O N A I R E 	S Y S T E M #
first generation millionaires and multi-millionaires.
Thatmeanstheydidnotinherittheirwealth.Nor,incidentally,didtheymarryit(alaJohnKerry).Theydid
notstartinbusinesswithapotofmoney.Mostcomefrommodestbackgrounds,manyfromoutrightpoverty—
growing up in homes with no indoor plumbing ,or in inner city ghettos, or other similar circumstances.
Almost all started and built businesses from scratch.
That means they did not begin by stepping into afamily business -the few in this group who did, turned
tiny, ordinary businesses into much more exciting, much more profitable businesses. They did not purchase
already established companies. And most started in their first businesses with little or no directly relevant edu¬
cation or apprenticeship. Many came from mundane jobs: car mechanic, hotel busboy, waitress, and so on.
Most have focused on 'renegade' business 'models.'
Many have, by deliberate strategy and preference, kept their businesses 'small', in terms of numbers of
employees and money captive in buildings, equipment or infrastructure, even in terms of gross sales —yet
NOT in terms of net profit taken home. Alot of these entrepreneurs Iwork with have homebased businesses
doing amillion to afew million dollars ayear, while some have bigger businesses —as many as 25 or 30
stores or offices, as much as 20 or 30-million dollars ayear. Afew have built businesses doing 100-million to
as much as 800-million ayear. But most are the small operators by choice. In all cases, their bottomline profits
are extraordinary by conventional standards.
As example, one, who does over 3-million ayear, from his home office, with two employees at another
location, keeps 2-million net. He also takes 12 weeks of vacation during the year. He is not arare bird in my
group;heisthe'underground'norm.ButunliketheThomasStanley/MillionaireNextDoorcrowd,mine
engagein"unusual"businessesor,atleast,applying"unusual"businessmodelstoseeminglyordinarybusi¬
nesses. While some of their other behaviors about money mimic what you read about in Stanley's research.
However, I'd quickly add, nothing they do is outside	their businesses are definitely NOT "next-door ordinary,
the reach or use of ANYONE now reading this letter.

SHOCKED to discover how little they have in common. That's why it is so very, very difficult to accurately
identify the very few things that really matter. They look different, act different, talk different, are of vastly dif¬
ferent ages, different backgrounds, different religions, different interests. Within the 30 or so Inow work with
most closely and continuously there are: very flamboyant extroverts, very quiet, even shy introverts. In their
20's, 30's, 60's. Some married to the same person for 20 years or longer, others 'swinging singles.' At least one,
married, a'swinger.' Catholic, protestant, metaphysical/new age. Interested in: fly fishing, another in golf,
another with zero interest in or knowledge of any sports. Some super-techies, some who need help turning on
alight switch. Afew with advanced college degrees, afew more who barely escaped high school. Some who
tell off-color jokes, others who are offended by them. Some who love fine wines and caviar, others who prefer
acheeseburger and beer. Cautious, bold. Wrestling with guilt, nearly without conscience. Hilarious, nearly
absent asense of humor. Workaholic, incredibly lazy. Icould fill another twenty pages with their differences,
but those would be twenty very unproductive pages. The point is, while they have very, very, very little in com¬
mon. the few commonalities they do share are critically, vitally important, profoundly significant, and when
understood, make major contribution to the 'blueprint' Ipromise you: the blueprint for for achieving whatever
goals whatever business/money/success/fame/impact and influence goals you may have, while absolutely
defying all normal, common, ordinary boundaries and restrictions -on speed, ease, independence.
Before we're done. I'll return to this subject, and give you something of a"peek" into these commonalities.
And Istress, again, as Iwill again, what Itell you about their few but vital commonalities is NOT based
mere observation, interview or research, but on sleeves rolled up, hands dirty, in the trenches, up close and per¬
sonal work with these people, on project after project, day in, day out, for years.
o n
Which brings me to the SECOND CATEGORY of source material for my Renegade Millionaire System:
me. My life.

youjustcan'tbethatimportantaperson,thatyoumustannoyeveryoneinyourwake,byconstantly,mcessant-

I've seen afather in his front yard trying to play catch with his son and talk on his cellphone. He should be
horsewhipped. In front of his son, so the kid learns something. Inow routinely see men in public restrooms
standing at urinals, peeing and talking on their cellphones simultaneously. Friend, if you can't pee in peace, opt
for abullet between the eyes and put yourself and everyone around you out of misery.
Ifeel even greater pity and sadness for the even bigger fools who feel compelled to answer anyone and
everyone who might call them on their cellphones, anytime, anywhere, 24-7. Asses, all. If you are perceived
by your clients, boss, or marketplace as that pathetic an interchangeable commodity, that you live in dire fear
of losing apiece of business by not being instantly and constantly accessible, you've got gigantic problems that
can only get worse, until no cellphone, no instant, eager response can solve them.
You are adead man talking, and you're too dumb to know it.
Not only don't Iown one of these infernal things, Itake virtually no unscheduled phone calls at all. Even
clients paying me $100,000.00 or more ayear make advance appointments to talk with me on the phone -and
those are kept to aminimum, as Iprefer getting FAXes. Itend to "gang" all
the necessary phone appointments into aday or two every two or three
weeks. This lets me work (or play) ALL the rest of the time 100% free of
interruptions, work stoppages, annoyances. (I also do not use e-mail, inci¬
dentally.)

Everybody in every business I'm in says this cannot be done. Pfui.
Ahandful of years back, Iwas traveling more than Lewis &Clark.
Now Itravel sparingly. Ire-engineered my entire business, radically, to make
this change. Iwalked away from over $500,000.00 ayear in income to do it.
But Ifilled that vacuum Icreated with more money made to suit my prefer¬
ences within ayear. Now Iactually make clients come to -even put entire
conventions in -Cleveland (!!!) ,so Iwill be available to them.
Ifire troublesome clients. Again, I'd quickly emphasize, this is nothing
new. Idid it when Icould ill afford it too, because Iinnately understood I
could ill afford not to. As aresult, today, Iwork only with people Ienjoy
working with, period, no exceptions. Ihave as recently as last month, paid out $20,000.00 to rid myself of a
'mistake'; aclient who was causing me stress.
It has been years since I've sold myself or my services. Oh, I'm still sometimes asked; someone wants ref¬
erences, or me to 'pitch' myself to him or his company's decision-making committee. Ialways refuse. It's my
strongest belief that ANYONE —Realtor, dog groomer —can put themselves in exactly this same position.
Ihave raised my fees, overall, by nearly 400% in the last 3years. In one area of my work, Iam now charging
more than famous, even legendary peers with two to three times my experience. Yet Iturn away as much work
a s I t a k e a n d a m o v e r b o o k e d .
Ilive pretty much as Ichoose. If Iwant to take mornings to go out to the track and train my horses, days
off to go to afair where one of the young horses is racing, Ido. Iwork by choice with no staff underfoot; in
fact, my sole staffperson is in an office Iset foot in fewer than six times last year. We talk for under an hour
three or four days aweek by phone. If you go back to the Autonomy List -which Isuggest you keep -1 score
between a'9' and a'10', on a1-10 scale, on every single one of the items listed. Don't envy me. Join me.

NEWToThisYear’sInfo-SUMMIT...
N-D-E-D
Staff Training Session(Hosted by Robert Skrob, President of the Information Marketing Association, with Michelle O’Halloran, Vice-
President of Operations at Glazer Kennedy Insider’s Circle™)
Robert 	conducted 	one 	of 	these 	just 	for 	our 	Platinum, 	Info-Mastermind,
and 	VIP 	Members’ 	‘key’ 	staff 	last 	year 	and 	it 	was 	such 	aHUGE 	success 	we
decided 	to 	offer 	it 	again 	at 	this 	year’s 	Info-Summit. 	This 	Session 	focuses
on 	sharing 	‘best 	practices’ 	for 	customer 	service, 	vendor 	management.
Juggling 	competing 	priorities, 	handling 	events, 	and 	much 	more. 	If 	you 	have
staff, 	bring 	‘em 	with 	you 	and 	have 	them 	participate 	in 	this 	special 	5-hour
session 	and 	attend 	the 	rest 	of 	the 	Info-SUMMIT 	and 	day 	before A-Z
Blueprint 	Seminar 	Day. 	(Just 	register 	them 	for 	half 	price 	and 	have 	them
attend 	the 	Staff 	Training 	and 	the 	balance 	of 	the 	entire 	event 	with 	you.)
Your staff will gain practical insights, little known implementation secrets and mastermind opportunities that could
only be possible by spending time with other exceptional info-marketing staff.
This program will be delivered by Robert Skrob of the Information Marketing Association. For his entire career
Robert has been THE IMPLEMENTATION guy for his company. As an employee, Robert’s sole focus was building
systems, communicating with clients and motivating staff with accountabiiity. He’s personally hired and trained dozens of
employees, managed the billing and fulfillment for dozens of companies and currently oversees the systems that fulfills the
administrative needs of over 22 companies. He’s been in the same situations your staff now faces and his “lessons
learned” will be extremely valuable to them.
Now, after 8years of Glazer-Kennedy training he brings an understanding of the info-marketing business and
marketing tactics that generate and keep customers. This allows him to deliver real ‘hands-on’ experience, with your needs
in mind like no other management consultant you could bring in. Plus, he’s an implementer so he’s intimately familiar with
many of the software tools currently employed within your business and in some cases can offer “already-done” systems
your staff can implement within your business.
Plus, your staff will benefit from each other. Quite frankly, there is no one person that could answer the range of
questions that comes up within info-marketing businesses. However, through this program your staff can ask questions to
dozens of “experts” in info-marketing application by interacting with other staff members in this session. Your staff will
benefit for many years from the contacts they make in this session..

work got done.

Let’s talk abit more about this dynamic duo...
Michael Taggart, also known by the name of Michael X, has been in the SEO industry for more
than thirteen years, building his first site when he was only twenty-two years of age. He began in 1999,
and has been recognized for his contribution to many prestigious online marketing careers as acoach,
and through coaching programs.
Michael is the CEO of Adventure Marketing EEC, and works alongside astaff of dedicated employees
through this Boise, Idaho-based firm. Michael is also CEO of Rock Steady, Inc. and is trained in local
and international businesses, and this has helped hone his online marketing strategies throughout his
c a r e e r .
One of the most renowned marketers in the world, especially in terms of local search marketing and
mobile SEO, his interests include Bitcoin and crypto assets, giving him areputation for technologically
advanced knowledge, and the title of “the coolest marketer in America.”
Mike Stewart is president and founder of Soundpages Inc., acomplete multimedia production
facility located in the Atlanta area of Georgia, USA. Mike has composed countless radio jingles,
television scores, and produced or performed on music heard all over the world. He has worked with
world famous musicians such as Tommy Roe, Joe South, The Shirelles, William Bell (Georgia Hall of
Fame Recipient), Isaac Hayes, Eddie Floyd, Bertie Higgins, and Bill Anderson: The list goes on and on
of Georgia performers who’s who. He owned and operated arecording studio for over twenty years
working in all facets of digital audio and video.
In 1996, Mike became passionate about the future of the Internet and how small businesses could benefit
from the web if they just understood how to make it work for their company. He saw how the broadcast
of audio and video information on awebsite was no different than the television/ radio industry he had
been apart of all his life.
Since that time, he has become very successful consulting with small business owners on how to
broadcast their unique marketing message to the world. He is always on the lookout for new innovative
ideas, products, hardware and software to benefit his clients.
IMPORTANT: You do NOT need to be atechno-whiz-kid to make this work in your business.
Taggart and Stewart will show you how INSANELY EASY it is to use cutting-edge technology to
leapfrog light years ahead of everyone else in your market.
It doesn't matter what business you're in, this is one Bonus Day you can NOT afford to miss. Best of all,
it’s just $197 for Diamond, $247 for Gold, and your guest can come along for $97.

Listings of Chapter Meetings on Reverse Side 	“A key concept for me, and much
of what Ifocus on as an IBA and
as national sales and marketing
consultant, is the concept of
segmentation andfocus in
marketing. The big breakthroughs
have come by not trying to target
everyone and by creating specific,
focused marketing messages to select
audiences. It’s not really abig secret
but Inotice the great majority of
marketers nowadays still try to be all
things to all people. By segmen ting
my marketing Ihave become much
more relevant to my market which
in turn allows me to stand out more
and naturally be that much more
effective. ’’

equal.” Maybe so.. .but Ican tell you from my 30+ years of experience on the business

are NOT created equal.”

based on price. Never!! If you’re serious about making money, surely you’d want to know
EVERYTHING you possibly could about those customers -WHO are they? WHERE are they?
HOW can you reach them? There’s literally afortune to be made in attracting AFFLUENT
customers, clients, or patients and if you want to discover the keys to the vault, you’ll take a
few minutes to read this important message.

clients on developing methods to attract the customers, clients, or patients from the top rungs of
the economic ladder- the ones who have the money and are willing to spend it -and after years
of testing, tweaking, and experimentation in my “marketing lab” I’ve discovered the ‘secret
formula’ -the keys that unlock the vault to virtually unlimited riches.

IFINALLY spilled the beans on everything I’ve discovered about marketing to the fast-growing
AFFLUENT, MASS AFFLUENT, and ULTRA-AEELUENT markets at alive event in
Cleveland to asmall group of people who paid $5,000.00 to discover my closely-guarded
‘secrets’ to attracting wealthy customers, clients, and patients. This Marketing to the
AFFLUENT Workshop will NEVER be repeated, but the good news is it was professionally
recorded so it’s not too late to discover the ‘secrets’ of finding and selling to those who are
least and last affected hy price (and don’t worry -the investment required for these valuable

more on that (PLUS three Valuable Bonuses) in aminute.
Here's 	What 	This 	is 	All 	About...
This is all about you targeting the ideal customers, clients, and patients that are not price
or fee resistant. Isee too many business owners, entrepreneurs, and marketers making the
HUGE MISTAKE of fighting over aclass of consumers that has little disposable income to
begin with.. .and most of that is spent on basic staples like food and shelter leaving little left for
other things. They shop at The Dollar Store or Wal-Mart, eat at Taco Bell or Denny’s, and stay
at the Budget Inn or the Motel 6. Their buying decisions are heavily influenced by price.
At the polar opposite of the spectrum is another “super” class of consumers -the AFFLUENT
new “Spending Class” -that has money and is ready, willing, and able to spend it on
“premium” eoods and services. They shop at Nordstrom and Saks Fifth Avenue, eat at
Morton’s and Ruth’s Chris, and stay at the Four Seasons and the Ritz-Carlton. Price is virtually
irrelevant to this EXPLODING class of consumers. They want something else, and if you can
provide it to them, they’ll pay almost any price. (Keep reading to find out what they want).
The gap between these two classes is widening. It’s hard enough to eke out aliving selling to the
“Wal-Mart” class (nothing against Wal-Mart, Ioccasionally shop there as well).. .and during
challenging economic times it gets even more difficult to sell goods and services to them. The
problem is that most businesses are competing on price and there’s ALWAYS going to be some
idiot who comes around and sells your ‘widget’ for alower price. Competing on price is the
equivalent of abusiness death sentence. (If you need proof, look at all the small local
businesses that were wiped out by “big box” stores like Wal-Mart, Best Buy, Men’s Wearhouse,
etc. who rolled into town offering the same merchandise at lower prices). To make matters
worse, the “Wal-Mart” class of consumers clamps up their wallets tighter than Ebenezer Scrooge
when the economy heads south. On the other hand, the AFFLUENT spending class is almost
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

immune to recession and certainly they are the LEAST and LAST affected bv economic
t u r b u l e n c e .
Isn’t it obvious which customers, clients,or patients are most PROFITABLE? Would you rather
scratch and claw for every sale?Are you sick and tired of riding the economic roller coaster
wherebusinessisOKduringgoodtimesanddriesuplikeaghosttownduringbadtimes?
Wouldn’titbeawisestrategicbusinessdecisiontoshiftyourfocustoattractingAFFLUENT

price is NOT the determining factor? Of course it would and if you keep reading I’ll explain

WHY is this New "Spending Class" of
A F F L U E N T 	C o n s u m e r s 	E - X - P - L - O - D - I - N - S ?
Good question. And here’s the simple answer: We are in the midst of ademographic revolution,
with the peak of the Baby Boomer population, the emerging MASS-AFELUENT and other
trends and changes -all converging to produce anew “Spending Class” of consumers that
need to be understood and targeted -by you! Contrary to all the doom ‘n gloom you’re
bombarded with daily by atainted media hell bent on selling recession, something unique,
dynamic, and potentially enormously beneficial has been occurring, is occurring right now, and
promises to continue for the next 10 years...which could be THE income, wealth, and qualitv-of-
business-life breakthrough for you. It is the EXPLOSION OF AFFLUENCE in the U.S. and
abroad.. .the BOOM in luxury spending.. .the SKYROCKETING DEMAND for “premium”
services and cheerful willingness to pay for them.
This ‘special buying behavior’ is concentrated in aminority -but avery significant minority -of
consumers.. .and IF you know WHO they are, WHERE they are, WHAT they desire, WHAT
they respond to and HOW to effectively connect your business with them, you can prosper
WILDLY, like never before! (this is the TOP PRIORITY that I’ve been working on with my
private clients for the past several years. Now I’ll gladly guide you on this ULTRA-
PROEITABLE 	path 	too).
So WHERE is the Money?
Did you know that 22% of US households have over 55% of all earned income? In other words:
MORE than half the income is concentrated in one of five households. Surely it must be
easier to profit sell to the 1in 5than to the 4in 5. Think about it. Do you want to be one of the
MASSES of businesses marketing to the nearly 80% of US households with LESS than half the
money hoping you’ll be the ‘lucky’ one that gets their business? If so, you’re no smarter than
the millions of ‘mathematically-challenged’ people who buy lottery tickets every week hoping
they’ll be the lucky winner. Hoping is NOT agood strategy for success.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 340 of 830 --

Instead of being lost in acrowd of “me-too” marketers targeting the majority of the consumers
who have the minority of the money, wouldn’t it be abetter strategy to take acontrarian
approachandbeoneofthefewsmartmarketerstargetingtheminorityofconsumerswhocontrol
themajorityofthemoney?ThoseforwhompriceisNOTthedeterminingfactor?Those
LEAST and LAST affected by economic turbulence?
Pssst...I’m going to let you in on aMAJOR SECRET:
WHO" Your Marketing Message Attracts Determines Your
Income 	and 	Wealth 	More 	Than 	ANY 	Other 	Factor...
%%
The MARKET (in other words the “WHO”) and the MESSAGE are two sides of my famous
Marketing triangle -in case you’re wondering, MEDIA is the third. Your marketing message is
the bait that attracts prospects and the “MESSAGE to MARKET match” is avital piece of the
puzzle I’ve been preaching for decades -but many marketers still don’t get it. For example, if
you want to attract deer, you shouldn’t be upset, surprised or disappointed to attract mice and rats
if you use cheese as the bait when you should be using asalt lick.
What does this have to do with you? Plenty!
You can NOT use the same old marketing message to attract AFFLUENT customers,
clients, and patients. You’ll need to change your bait, use adifferent marketing message. The
marketing message that attracts people to Saks Fifth Avenue is different than the message that
attracts crowds to Wal-Mart. The message that works to fill the Budget Inn will NOT sell rooms
a t t h e R i t z - C a r l t o n .
IMPORTANT! Before you can attract the AFFLUENT, you MUST understand them and
if you would chop off your own hand with adull axe before you would hand over your credit
card to buy an $800.00 golf club or pay $6,000.00 to fly somewhere on aprivate jet when you
could get there on Southwest for $49.00 or fork over $1,200.00 for an elegant dining experience
when you could feed an entire family for amonth for the same price, then you do not understand
the rich and are therefore banned from success in selling to them. Ican save you years of
frustration, hassle, and wasted time &money by showing you EXACTLY what bait attracts
these AFFLUENT customers, clients, and patients.
TWO More Ways to Profit by
Attracting More AFFLUENT Customers...
There are only afew ways to make more money with your current business. One of them is to
INCREASE the average transaction size. By attracting AFFLUENT customers, clients, and
patients for whom price is NOT the determining factor, you can increase your prices. And that
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 341 of 830 --

would automatically increase the average transaction size and increase your profits. Think about
it. Would you rather be selling $100 widgets or $500 widgets or $1,000 widgets? When you
start attracting the right prospects (like I’ll show you), you can easily increase the prices of your
widgets and enjoy more PROFITS. This allows you to invest more money to attract prospects (a
HUGE advantage over your competitors) and it takes fewer sales to make the same profit
when you raise your prices. You’d have to sell 10 times more $100 widgets to make
$100,000.00 than you would if you were selling $1,000 widgets.
The second great reason to attract more AFFLUENT customers is referrals. Areferral is the
second easiest customer to sell (after an active customer). Referrals are predisposed to do
business with you because they’ve been referred to you by someone they already know, like, and
trust. And the best part is that AFFLUENT consumers are more likely to patronize businesses
their friends and family refer them to. BUT.. .there are ‘secrets’ to getting AFFLUENT

“Looking back over the years (since 1996) that Ihave known you and worked with your materials, there has been adramatic
improvement in my family's financial situation, which revolves around my CPA firm. Here’s some of the benefits we have
enjoyed. In anutshell, aCPA firm that was struggling for survival is now profitable and prosperous. It’s nice to be selective
about the people that we serve. Ihave had the financial freedom to fire several “problem" clients. Ihave the confidence of
knowing Ican “open up our faucet’’ at will. My wife and Iwere able to buy two new cars for cash. We have upgraded our
home, Ihave been making regular deposits to my retirement accounts (formerly asimplified employee pension plan, now a
401 (k) plan.). My wife and Ihave enjoyed vacations to Europe, Hawaii, Cancun, Ireland, aMexican cruise, the Canadian and
U.S. northeast, Washington, D.C. and have now scheduled avacation to Scotland, all debt-free.
For anyone who reads this letter and has abusiness or is asalesperson, you will find you have made an excellent, profitable
decision when you make ahabit of studying and implementing Dan Kennedy’s materials. When you buy Dan’s materials and
become an Insider’s Circle member, it is not an expense, but an investment in yourself.’’
Michael C. Gray, CPA; San Jose, CA

A b o u t 	P o o r 	v s . 	R i c h
-poor V. rich thinking, poor v. rich behavior, poor v. rich business strategy
ThereARE four kinds of people who DO attend our Info-SUMMIT^'^ -with one shared commonality.
There are lots of reasons NOT to attend, but most peoples’ fit three categories. Td like to touch on all
these items as briefly as possible, and hopefully help you make afirm, fast decision you feel good about.
/also want to give you something in exchange for your time reading this -a“success factor” linked to
time management, personal productivity, personal value and worth, and the way that the highest income
earners are able to accomplish so much more than others. I’ll get to that later in this letter. DON’T
MISS 	IT. 	READ 	EVERY 	WORD, 	IT 	IS 	SOMETHING 	I’VE 	NOT 	ENUNCIATED 	BEFORE.
F i r s t . . . . w h o c o m e s - a n d s h o i d d c o m e ?
ll Well-established, seasoned, smart, successful Info-Marketers. Authors. Speakers. Publishers and
Event Promoters, because with big income businesses, even the tiniest turn of the screw matters. In a$5-
million ayear business, a1% boost puts $50,000.00 in one’s pocket. Find five of those and you make an
extra $250,000.00. They know it and they come to hunt, for the turn of the screw, the new contact, the

with friends... AND abusiness? There’s just not enough time in the day, and something’s gotta give,
right? We’ve got people that have dealt with exactly that problem and will tell them how they did it.

HAVE ATEMPERATURE?
Has the recent economy given you aheadache, indigestion, acid reflux, insomnia? Are you burn¬
ing up brain cells at an alarming rate, figuring out your next moves? Does the news of the day make
blood squirt from your ears? How about an “opportunity, perspective and vision pill?
Who better to deliver the best medicine -
LAUGHTER
-than	superstar
JOAN	RIVERS!But let’s not underestimate Joan as “just acomic’’ -although she certainly is
one ofTHE most successful female comedians of all time, ground-breaking
when she began in days of comedy as aboys’ club; famously caustic, acerbic,
and ruthless with take-no-prisoners comedian that has kept her in the public
eye, much talked about, much criticized, and much loved by fans for more
than 35 years.
J O A N R I V E R S I S A P H E N O M E N A L E N T R E P R E N E U R
PRO 	who has built abusiness empire from scratch on the strength of her
personality, intuition, innovation, drive, determination. Her company controls some of
the most successful brands and product lines sold on the #1 home-shopping channel
QVC, where she can be seen frequently, performing “live,” without anet, aconsummate
master of one-to-many selling. This business began literally on acard-table, with jewelry
in cardboard boxes, with Joan and one associate doing everything in 1990 -it has far
surpassed $200-million in sales. She damn near willed it into existence! In addition,
she presides over abusiness encompassing authorship, professional speaking, Broadway
play, film and television projects fueled by her incessant reinvention of self and boundless
creativity.
SHE DEMONSTRATED HER INGENUITY AND TOUGHNESS on national TV, over 15 weeks of intense
competition on the 2009 edition of Donald Trump’s CELEBRITY APPRENTICE. At age 75, Joan out-thought,
out-schemed, out-worked, out-fought acollection of contestants....culminating in aclimactic battle with professional poker
champion Annie Duke, in abusiness task fraught with unexpected adversity that had to be quickly overcome....then the final
Boardroom showdown on “live” TV. From the outset, Donald Trump thought it might come to their fight; he said, “They’re both
tough. They're both smart. And they both hate each other.” The victory produced a$250,000.00 contribution for Joan’s charity,
G o d ’ s L o v e W e D e l i v e r.
JOAN ISA SURVIVOR. In 1987, her husband Edgar committed suicide, and she lost her late-night talk show on FOX -
which helped launch that network, but destroyed her long-time, close relationship with Johnny Carson, for whom she’d been
his only repeat female guest host. For atime, there was no open door for Joan as acomedienne or actress, and she wound
up re-starting her public career at the lowest level, appearing on ‘Hollywood Squares.’ working for scale. She turned it all
into fodder for serious writing and lecturing on coping with and recovering from adversity, tragedy, and grief, and for two of
her three bestselling non-fiction books. Bouncing Back and Still Talking. Joan is an inspiring example of mental toughness, self-
motivation and persistence we can all benefit from, in these challenging times.
S U P E R - S A L E S
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

T h e 	a n s w e r : 	P o w e r 	P o i n t s .
Let me explain how this tool was built.
Avery capable editor and organized-thinker, very familiar with my work, took everything we could find that /had said,
taught, recorded, or written, published and unpublished, even consulting session notes, since 1975...quite literally atrailer-truck
full of raw material... and set out to edit out all the duplication, pull together all the gems, and get it organized into categories and
disciplines, somewhat like an encyclopedia. It took her nearly an entire year of very dedicated effort. Iinvested alarge sum of
money in her efforts. Now I’ve been working with what she delivered, to make it even more useable and accessible.
In total, 33 years of my experience and nearly 3years of arduous labor have gone into assembling POWER POINTS for
you. Here was my goal, my idea: At any time, for any specific purpose, you could go right to the proper place in this tool and get
aquick, concise refresher— what would Dan Kennedy think and say about this? So, let’s say you’re getting ready to go and give
aspeech, to persuade 10 or 10,000 to accept your proposition or buy your products. You could instantly yank me into the room
and get aquick download of my best advice and strategies for group selling, platform selling, and public speaking.

instantly beam down into the chair next to vour desk and reel off my very best suggestions for building the most powerful,
m a g n e t i c o f f e r s p o s s i b l e .

-zap -there Iam, to go over ahandful of key ideas about winning in negotiations.

'7 starred reading Dan Kennedy stuff about 2years ago.
Iwas intrigued and believed things might work for some
businesses and that others might have success with
these type of marketing concepts, hut "MY BUSINESS
WAS DIFFERENT. ”LOUU Ikept reading though. I
started to realize that Dan seemed wbelieve many of
the same business principles that Ibelieve it takes to
he successful. Ialso noticed that he really did work his
own system, which shows me integrity, actually doing
what you tell others to do? WOW!
Ithen decided Iwould dabble in afew
Kennedy-style marketing campaigns. Iremember
putting my very first direct mail piece in the mail I
had just spent 2days stamping, writing addresses on
envelopes and giving them to the postmaster. Ireally
had my doubts. Iremember thinking...this will never
work, Iam wasting my money Holy cow was Iwrong.
Iproceeded to get the best respon.se ever from any
advertising campaign. The local chapter meetings with
Richelle and the inter action with the group gives me
the motivation to do different and exciting things and
most importantly to IMPLEMENT. My partner and I
both recommend Dan Kennedy to nearly every business
owner we .speak with. "

Ryan Spelts
GKIC/Las Vegas
"I want to share some exciting news. After you
originally introduced your mini-video camera several
months back at the Chapter Meeting Iwent out, got
one and started taping Pestimonlals (as Inamed
it). Iwanted to get them displayed on my website.
Through Glazer-Kennedy Insider’s Circle local chapter
meetings Imet aweb designer and programmer. I
recently decided to work with them and we implemented
video areas on my website. The way the program is
designed. Ican consistently add new videos and they
are on ahop. This is very exciting as it opens up new
opportunities for work as now potential customers can
see and listen to other people using our service.
This all came about by being amember of
your local chapter and exposing me to other creative
marketers as me. Iam extremely excited to the
connections Icontinue to make at your GKIC meetings
