/am amember of Chris Hum’s GKIC Mastermind group. At our January meeting,
Chris asked each of us to recap our business accomplishments for 2007. Iwas not too
eager to review my year. Last year was abig challenge for me. My revenue as aresidential
mortgage broker was way off from the previous year. In fact, the first few months of2007 were
rough, but the year would get worse -much worsel
On the evening of June 20th my son. Rod Jr., was involved in aserious car accident.
His injuries were life threatening. He suffered abrain injury, partial paralysis, and nerve
damage to his eyes. He fought like achampion, never giving up his will to live. Rod was
eventually released from ORMC after several weeks of extensive rehabilitation. His recoveiy
was slow and methodical, requiring 24-hour supervision. Iadjusted my .schedule to work from
home and be with him. As things turned out. 1would never set foot back in my mortgage office
again. It has been almost seven months since my son saccident. He continues to make great
strides towards afull recovery.
One Miracle Brings Forth Another...Looking back, Icannot imagine where my life would be
right now if not for my participation in Chris Hum sMastermind Coaching Group.
Ihave learned so much from Chris's mentorship and our group participants. Each member
has had apositive impact on my business development.
The additional time at home with my son allowed me to apply tremendous focus on
the GKIC marketing strategies taught in our coaching program. Iread eveiy book and article
Icould get my hands on, slowly developing my copywriting .skills. As my writing improved, I
began to take on small projects from members within our group. Within afew months, Iwas
writing extensive marketing campaigns for several business owners. By the end of the year,
anew career had emerged. As one door slammed closed, another door had slowly opened
-providing me with apassionate opportunity to pursue my ambition as acopywriter and
Our Philadelphia Chapter adopted a
locai charity to help with their fund
raising and previously the most
they raised was $27,000. With The
Chapter’s help they set agoal of
$35,000. Unfortunately, they didn’t
reach $35,000, they actually received
$104,000. See exhibits 14 &15 for
d e t a i l s .
e n t r e p r e n e u r.
What began as aterrible year, turned out to be one of the most exciting
opportunities of my life. Iam very grate:ful for Chris Hum's mentorship and the support and
friendship of each member of our group. Without them, Ican only imagine where my life (and
career) would be today.
Chapter Meetings are open to all Glazer-Kennedy
Insider’s Circle™ Members and your guests. In most
areas, you may attend one meeting free. If there is
no Advisor in your area and you would be interested
in becoming aNo B.S. Certified Business Advisor,
contact Howard Zeiden at 410-825-8600.

Yes, we’re pulling out all the

h e r i t s L i f e t i m e A c h i e v e m e n t A w a r d i n 2 0 0 5 .
As an entrepreneur. Suzanne has developed skin and
care products, Somersize food products, FaceMaster,
acquired and owns ThighMaster and other fitness
products, and in 2006, launched her own direct
sales/party plan sales company somewhat following the
Tupperware® and Mary Kay® business models, but
with astrong online marketing component.
While others may quake and
cower in fear of recession, the
savvy info-marketers that
populate Planet Dan laugh in
its face and spit in its eye -
and so do we! So, we’re
investing more than ever in
assembling the grandest Info-
SUMMIT^^ever.... Suzanne is also aknowledgeable and dedicated health
care advocate, recipient of numerous awards and honors
including the Humanitarian Award from the National
Council on Alcoholism, and she is the founder of the
S u z a n n e S o m e r s I n s t i t u t e f o r t h e E f f e c t s o f A d d i c t i o n o n
F a m i l i e s .
...beginning with Super-Star SUZANNE
S O M E R S .
Suzanne’s stardom began with her star-turn in the now-
classic movie ‘American Graffiti’ in 1973, soon
followed by her 5years on ‘Three’s Company,’ one of
the most highly rated and popular TV sitcoms of its
time, followed by 7years starring with Patrick Duffy in
‘Step By Step.’
Suzanne and her husband Alan Hamel, abrilliant
entrepreneur and marketing innovator, have been
together for 40 years, have two sons, adaughter and six
grandchildren. They work together as business partners.
overseeing manufacturing and marketing operations
encompassing over 1,000 products, as well as Suzanne’s
career as an entertainer.	But Suzanne is far from “just another pretty
f a c e ” o n T V . . .
Suzanne comes to the Info-SUMMIT®'^ stage as a
dynamic speaker; successful author; highly effective
sales professional thriving in one of the toughest
selling environments, television (where everyone
watching holds a‘clicker’); and as avisionary
entrepreneur in multiple product categories utilizing
virtually every marketing media: online, offline, sales
force and broadcast. This is aunique opportunity to
see and hear from one of the most popular
personalities in entertainment who -unlike most -
has successfully leveraged her star power into
s u s t a i n e d b u s i n e s s s u c c e s s .
She has authored 17 books including 8New York Times
bestsellers, created ‘Somersizing’ diet and exercise

In afast-changing media environment, with an online environment where the rules-of-engagement are so fluid, it is easy to be seduced
^by shiny objects absent sound strategy for using them; distracted from the fundamental factors governing the attraction, ascension
and retention of buyers of information, education and association. It is dangerously easy to get far adrift from and even lose sight of
the foundational, fundamental ideas behind EVERY info-marketing success story....the “x-factors” that motivate those who invest in
information, education, advice, training, coaching, etc. In short, what makes people buy?
In afast-paced journey from the earliest advent of ‘the success business’ through The Great Depression to the present, Dan will share
examples from his private archives and research from behind closed doors, Dan will reveal strategies from his leading info¬
marketing clients’ radical recession-to-opportunity maneuvers as well as their adherence to The Most Reliable Principles.

Why Dan Kennedy &Bill Glazer Are Telling
Their Members To STOP Sending Christmas
Cards 	To 	Their 	Best 	Customers!!!
Dear Member and Friend,
We know the above headline might surprise you, but it’s true. We suggest that you
STOP sending Christmas Cards to your best customers.
Why? "M^iie4500c^t-cjscjuHngout-
Thanksgiving Promotion. We
h^c} 54 clients cespond
genec^tingovec}18,OOO.OO
in s^les. We'I!definitely
incce^se the numbet ofc^cds
lye m^i! next ye^t."
Because your best customers are also receiving
alot of Christmas Cards from alot of other people at
the same time including their family, friends, and a
bunch of other companies doing business with them.
In fact, the average person receives forty-two
Christmas Cards every year mdyour Christmas card
gets lost in the clutter with all the others.
But there is asolution that sets you apart from the clutter that can not only
demonstrate to your best customers that you care about them, but can also drive them to do
business with you. Here’s what we suggest you do. Instead of sending your best
customers aChristmas Card, we suggest you send these same customers aThanksgiving
Greeting Card.
After all, what better way to thank your best customers for being your best
customers than during Thanksgiving....the holiday where we give thanks? Plus, since
very few businesses send out Thanksgiving Cards, yours will stand out and get noticed.
This is the exact same mailing that Bill Glazer used to send out to his 15,000 best
clients of his Gage retail store that drove his business to record levels for the 4-days after
Thanksgiving.
So, here’s areal special opportunity: We have contacted the Greeting Card
Company and the Voice Broadcast provider and negotiated a“super deal” just for our
m e m b e r s .
Robert R^feld

W h y 	T h e 	G K I C 	I n f o - S U M M I T S M 	M a t t e r s
am a“lone ranger” and ahermit by
temperament, nature and preference, but I
still recognize the value of select fraternity,
and the unique opportunity of
spontaneous discovery that can only occur
in acharged environment like the Summit.
There have been too many times in my life
when Ihave quite literally, very
unexpectedly stumbled upon or been
grabbed by asmall piece of information or
idea that had immense importance and
value to me, by being in places like the
Summit (including the aforementioned
speaking conventions).
When Iwas very active in speaking, I
understood, from the beginning, that it
was important for me to be at the annual
convention of that industry, held by the
National Speakers Association, and Idid
not miss asingle one, over 15 years. As I
evolved in knowledge, experience and
success, the value of being there
changed. Not every session or speaker
interested me. Ialso became amarketer
to my peers, engaged in alliances with
peers, and that provided separate and
additional reasons for attending. But
even without that incentive, I
understood that it is both negligent
and arrogant NOT to be at and part of
the yearly gathering of one’s
profession. And every single year,
something beneficial and profitable
came about by that decision. There were
certainly years when going was
i n c o n v e n i e n t o r I w a s u n d e r e n o r m o u s
time pressure. Acouple years, financial
pressure. But Idid not skip, because I
considered it an essential part of being a
professional in that field -as did others.
You may or may not consider yourself a
professional info-marketer, or aspire to be
one. Choosing acasual approach, choosing
to be an amateur is your right, as long as it is
deliberate choice, not delusion. But if you
want to be apro at this, behavior matters,
and one of the things professionals do is
engage with their industry leaders and
peers at their conventions. Successful
mystery writers go to the mystery writers’
convention. Investors at the Buffett level all
gather at their own annual meeting. And so
The personal opportunity attending and
participating gives you is like no other. The
blinding flash may occur in ahallway, in an
overheard conversation, with an exhibitor,
in aseminar session, at the bar with a
newfound peer. It can't happen at home.
And the minute you start thinking you’re
too smart for the room, you substitute ego
for intellectual curiosity, aconceit that
always proves costly. I’ve been at this for 40
years. Tve seen alot of people get VERY
puffed up about their own genius and
importance soon after deflated, humbled
a n d b r o k e .
But the Info-SUMMIT’’^’ matters, beyond
your personal benefits, and Idare to
suggest you have an obligation to support
it. Certainly, no one else makes the kind of
investment GKIC does in producing this
industry convention. There are plenty of
cheaper seminars, many only, truthfully,
about selling coaching. But the only
convention treating this industry as an
industry, its members as professionals, is
the Info-SUMMIT^''"'*. An industry without
its annual convention around which leaders
coalesce and at which “new blood” is
encouraged and embraced and assisted, so
the Industry can thrive and grow, is just not
an industry at all. Strength comes through
o n .
It’s quite afew years back that Iput together
the first Info-SUMMIT'^h This industry was
and is wildly fragmented and unorganized. I
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 508 of 830 --

association. GKIC is essentially the
industry’s parent, providing aplace and an
opportunity for info-marketers of diverse
disciplines, business models, markets and
levels of success to meet up and find ways to
aid each other. The existence of such a
parent, and such agathering place and
platform as the Info-SUMMIT‘‘^*'^‘ matters
because -without it -the establishment of
new ‘best practices’, self-regulation, and
strategic alliances all falls by the wayside. We
need to come together and find ways to
strengthen the Summit and, for that matter,
the IMA -not weaken it.
people successful in their own businesses to
become “gurus” in their industries and
professions, and to change them. Each year,
the best and the newest starting their
journeys gather and it happens all over
again. It is SO MUCH MORE THAN a
seminar. Itake personal satisfaction in it.
It could matter to you more than you can
possibly imagine, looking at it from the
outside and from adistance. Particularly
now, atime of uncertainty, upheaval and
stress in so many kinds of businesses -a
new, different, better opportunity may be
just what the doctor would order if he knew
what to prescribe for you.	If you have not yet registered to attend -
and if you have relevant services to offer, if
you seek joint ventures, etc., to exhibit as
well -Iwant you to think long and hard
about everything you’re NOT doing.
Obviously, you are neglecting personal
opportunity. You are also not supporting
your industr}^ you are not utilizing the best
opportunity to meet and form relationships
with peers, and you are jriot acting
professionally. You are standing apart from

Yes, smart marketing reduces the need for and stress of selling. But.
Still. At some point, somebody’s gotta sell something. I’ve been
selling my entire life, starting in late teens. Came up eating only
what Ikilled -and going hungry if Ilet the day’s prey get away.
What Ihave come to understand over 35+ years is that knowing
how to sell and being able to plan, craft and deliver effective sales
presentations is the source of total self-confidence and virtually
u n l i m i t e d i n c o m e .
This knowledge is needed whether selling one to one, face to face,
nose to nose, toes to toes or one to many, from the front of the
room or via media, such as webinars, teleseminars or sales copy.
Yet even many high income sales professionals are “winging it”
with little understanding of and less use of architectural process
or psychological sophistication -they are essentially running a
numbers game rather than an operating system. Also, geez, have
folks forgotten how to cancel out objections and close? Imean, really
close. Take no prisoners closing. Well, 1haven’t.

Irecently completed three days of fairly intense work with about 200 of our Members at
my special seminar, the 7-Figures Academy. Preparing for and conducting something like
that forces me to slow down, step out of my own day to day rush and routine, re-visit my
archives, and think, think, think....
August 10, 7:14 A.M.
Dear Friend,
One of the things Iwas very much reminded of was the remarkable endurance o/certain
powerful principles of success. Despite all the transformative change in our world -
from walking around with little handheld computers and all the instant communication
power they provide to something as utterly taken for granted as air travel -still, still, I
found myself preparing to talk about principles and examples of their application from
30, 50, even 100 years ago as well as today. Isuppose to some this sounds tedious.
They’re foolish to think so, yet it’s understandable. But to some, it arouses intense
curiosity -as it should.
Nowhere is this idea more significant than in the information marketing field, where I
live and work most of the time, have made my own fortune and made fortunes for clients,
guided so many others to fortunes, been occupied for 30 years. It is very significant here,
now for three basic reasons. Why should you be interested in this? Only if you are
interested in possessing the most reliable keys to your own fortune, your own financial
l i b e r a t i o n .
First of all, you should understand that if you are in or enter the information
marketing business, you are in the media business. Not precisely in the same way that
Disney or Warner Brothers or apure publisher is, but in it nonetheless. Virtually
everything we sell and collect money for is media, and 90% of the way that we sell it is
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 549 of 830 --

with media. It is easy to confuse means with method, method with principle. So it is easy
to be lost in the media and to get so lost in it, you lose touch with the purpose of using it.
Easy for you to let your business become all about using media, rather than about the
core that is motivating and appealing to customers. And that danger expands
exponentially as the amount of media to which you have access expands.
At this year’s Info-SUMMIT®“, there’s an entire “boot camp within a
boot 	camp” 	devoted 	entirely 	to 	the 	most 	popular 	and 	emerging 	new
social media, and this is important; there is advantage to be had by
understanding 	the 	profitable 	opportunities 	each 	media 	offers,
disadvantage 	to 	mis-using 	it 	or 	to 	ignoring 	it.
But there is also danger in being so seduced by it you are tricked into thinking it is what
your business is about. It is not. You are in the media business but your business is not
about the media you use. Your business must be about some deep, profound, meaningful
connection with an audience eager and willing to support you and welcome your work on
acontinuous basis. That’s one of the things Iwant to talk with you about during my time
on stage at this year’s Info-SUMMIT
CHANGING, UNASSAILABLE TRUTHS -for exceptional success in info-marketing atANY
time, in ANY conditions.
Which brings me, second, to some cautionary comment about the present-moment
^economyandtheNewEconomytocome.Thereisenormoustemptationtothinkthat
“old” principles are made obsolete by economic change, and must be cast aside wholesale
in favor of some “success 2.0.” While surface changes in economic conditions, cultural
trends or even fads, social mores, as well as media often require adjustments, the idea
that the core reasons someone either attracts and sustains afollowing of interested,
enthusiastic supporters that makes him rich -or fails to do so -ever change is as
wrong-headed as wrong-headed can be.
duringwhichIwillpresentSEVENNEVER-
S M
Whether you like it or not, the exact same reasons that Napoleon Hill attracted such a
zealous following in the 1940’s, that Earl Nightingale rose from the obscurity of local
radio (and having to moonlight in insurance sales) to become the most influential and
celebrated voice of success and amulti-millionaire because of it in the 1960’s, and they
both have had strong, sustained interest in them fueling substantial businesses and
incomes decades after their deaths are the reasons you will rise or sink in the marketplace
today and tomorrow -and not understanding these core reasons leaves you
oh-so-vulnerable to superficial, fickle customers who may abandon you in
the blink of an eye for the most trivial of reasons or the most casual of seductions by
someone new. Iwant to talk candidly with you about these reasons when we meet at the
Info-SUMMIT^’^ this November. And Iintend to reveal some things about these reasons
that Ihave not spoken publicly and directly about before.
Bill asked, on your behalf, that Ireach deep in order to bring something to my time at this
year’s SUMMIT^"^ that is new and different than anything Ihave already put out.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 550 of 830 --

Ironically, that “new” will have profound relationship with “old.” Iwant to tell you, for
example, about aman living in atent in aDepression tent city, with amanual typewriter
as his only tool, who built agreat income and agreat life, and reveal to you an
unassailable truth demonstrated by his story, that offers you practical application right
now, in this economy, in your business. Ihave two secrets for you about what Dr.
Maxwell Maltz sold -that made him one of the two most popular, bestselling authors in
the self-improvement category of his time. (In inflation adjusted dollars, his book
royalties alone exceeded amillion dollars ayear.) These are “old” examples that offer
new insight. With this insight, you could so “sharpen the focus” of your impact on your
customers and prospects that your info-business income can multiply almost overnight.
More importantly, your business will be stronger and impervious to changing winds or
s t o r m s .
^fFinally, third, Iwant to emphasize that these core reasons why people invest or
Vdon’t 	invest 	in 	information, 	education, 	opportunities, 	why 	they 	ascend 	or 	don’t
ascend, why they implement or don’t, stay with you or don’t are far, far more
important and more powerful than techniques or technologies. The best tools in all
the world in the hands of someone who does not truly understand the philosophy behind
architecture, the influence abuilding has on its inhabitants and on the community around
it, the importance of environment on psyche and soul only briefly empower ahack to do
harm to himself and others -even if he might temporarily make some money and appear
successful. In short, there is aphilosophical component io exceptional, sustained success
as an info-marketer, regardless of the particulars of your information or niche. If this
sounds vague and ethereal, and you are only interested in being handed the simplest of
step 1, step 2, step 3instructions for making amillion, Isuppose I’m the wrong guy for
you and you the wrong person for me; Iam interested in working for amore thoughtful
entrepreneur. But don’t underestimate me either. Ican cut to the quick when it is
demanded of me. And within this third area of discussion, during my time at the Info-
SUMMIT^*^, Iwill pull out and hold up in the air so you can’t possibly miss it or
misinterpret it one statement, one statement, that defines the difference between those of
us who not only make alot of money in info-marketing but rise to the level of legendary
status and sustain that success and prominence, and those who never get much traction or
are here today but gone tomorrow.
As far as my presentation goes, Idon’t give arat’s tail if you Twitter or not. Right now I
have aclient making avery nice $100,000.00 amonth with no use of the
internet other than the simplest of web sites, otherwise direct-mail. Ihave other
clients using every online media there is and afew that haven’t been invented yet, and
making fortunes too. They have hardly anything in common with regard to technology.
What they do have in common is use of these core reasons that info-consumers buy.
And, if what you are most interested in now is afast path to aquick buck, Ipass no
critical judgment, and promise you can walk out after my presentation ‘armed ‘n
dangerous’ with afew fresh tricks that’ll materialize money from just about any customer
or prospect group, for just about any info-marketer. Heck, I’m even going to give you a
list of these. A“cheat sheet.” You can pick something off the list like picking your dinner

NEWToThisYear’sInfo-SUMMIT...

Staff Training Session(Hosted by Robert Skrob, President of the Information Marketing Association, with Michelle O’Halloran, Vice-
President of Operations at Glazer Kennedy Insider’s Circle™)
Robert 	conducted 	one 	of 	these 	just 	for 	our 	Platinum, 	Info-Mastermind,
and 	VIP 	Members’ 	‘key’ 	staff 	last 	year 	and 	it 	was 	such 	aHUGE 	success 	we
decided 	to 	offer 	it 	again 	at 	this 	year’s 	Info-Summit. 	This 	Session 	focuses
on 	sharing 	‘best 	practices’ 	for 	customer 	service, 	vendor 	management,
juggling 	competing 	priorities, 	handling 	events, 	and 	much 	more. 	If 	you 	have
staff, 	bring 	‘em 	with 	you 	and 	have 	them 	participate 	in 	this 	special 	5-hour
session 	and 	attend 	the 	rest 	of 	the 	Info-SUMMIT 	and 	day 	before A-Z
Blueprint 	Seminar 	Day. 	(Just 	register 	them 	for 	half 	price 	and 	have 	them
attend 	the 	Staff 	Training 	and 	the 	balance 	of 	the 	entire 	event 	with 	you.)
Your staff will gain practical insights, little known implementation secrets and mastermind opportunities that could
only be possible by spending time with other exceptional info-marketing staff.
This program will be delivered by Robert Skrob of the Information Marketing Association. For his entire career
Robert has been THE IMPLEMENTATION guy for his company. As an employee, Robert’s sole focus was building
systems, communicating with clients and motivating staff with accountability. He’s personally hired and trained dozens of
employees, managed the billing and fulfillment for dozens of companies and currently oversees the systems that fulfills the
administrative needs of over 22 companies. He’s been in the same situations your staff now faces and his “lessons
learned” will be extremely valuable to them.
Now, after 8years of Glazer-Kennedy training he brings an understanding of the info-marketing business and
marketing tactics that generate and keep customers. This allows him to deliver real ‘hands-on’ experience, with your needs
in mind like no other management consultant you could bring in. Plus, he’s an implementer so he’s intimately familiar with
many of the software tools currently employed within your business and in some cases can offer “already-done” systems
your staff can implement within your business.
Plus, your staff will benefit from each other. Quite frankly, there is no one person that could answer the range of
questions that comes up within info-marketing businesses. However, through this program your staff can ask questions to
dozens of “experts” in info-marketing application by interacting with other staff members in this session. Your staff will
benefit for many years from the contacts they make in this session..

From the Desk ofAJ. Mirabedini
My fellow entrepreneurs,
What athrill to be inviting you to Info-SUMMIT 2017, the premier information-marketing
event of the year. As CEO of one of the largest info-marketing organizations in the world, it
is ajoy and privilege to share not just the inner workings of this business but also its No. 1
Superhero, Dan Kennedy, the man who has made an industry from aconcept.
At GKIC, we are committed and focused on how best we can serve the business needs of
entrepreneurs around the world. We stand with you as you navigate the peaks and valleys
of life and business, all of us as acommunity focused on achieving success as we define
it. Information marketing is not just in our DNA; it's one of the clearest paths for you to
achieve your goals. I've seen it first hand, and I'm excited to have you experience the very
best of information-marketing strategies and resources this year in Cleveland.
Why Cleveland, you may ask? The answer Is simple: it's the home of Dan Kennedy, and
therefore the home of info-marketing. We felt there was no better place to share the
depths of this business model AND celebrate the greatest of the great than Cleveland.
We are bringing you the Super Heroes of Information Marketing right at the birthplace
of Superman, where it all got started. Dramatic? Maybe, but what agreat environment in
which to come together and learn from those who have pioneered and prospered in the
information-marketing world. Dan Kennedy, Bill Glazer, Robin Robins, Rich Schefren,
Ryan Levesque are some of the roots and new shoots of this ecosystem -and for three
days, you will be learning not just the how's but the why's of leveraging your deep career
and life knowledge into abusiness that will add adynamic dimension to your financial
position.
All of us at GKIC have exerted much toward recreating the magic that makes us the
destination and home for entrepreneurs aspiring to further success. Dan and Ihave
worked tirelessly to merge the past and the future, and Info-SUMMIT 2017 is where it all
comes together, in Cleveland.
Please join me there -Ican't wait to share it with you!
A.J. Mirabedini, CEO

FROM -Dan Kennedy: “It's The Place Where Fortunes Have Been Made -The Light Bulb In The Head
Went Off -Members Have Leaped Out Of Their Seats And Screamed I 	F I N A L L Y 	6 0 T 	I T ! ! "
Last Year 419 Inner Circle Members Came Together For
MARKETING and MONEY-MAKING Strategies From The Top
Experts In the World...And No One Left Disappointed!
Dear Inner Circle Member,
It’s been nearly eight months since we concluded SuperConference 2005 and Bill and Iare still receiving
letters and faxes from attendees telling us their personal story how they got one or ‘several’ ideas that have put huge
deposits into their bank accounts as aresult of attending this event.
As we are planning for this year’s event, there are several details that we already have nailed down and afew
that we would like to solicit your input on.
Here’s What We Already Know About SuperConference 2006:
The Event will be taking place March 30-April 2, 2006. We are currently in negotiations with acouple of hotels in
order to offer our members top notch facilities at reasonable rates.
At my urging Bill Glazer has agreed to deliver a‘one-of-a-kind’ full day presentation on ‘The Building Blocks and
Insider Secrets to Creating Copy That Sells ’that no one should miss, but many will have to because...

Ultimate Solutions Advertising
S e c r e t # 1 : M a r k e t i n g I s E v e r v t h i n g . . . H e r e * s W h y !
From the first day you got into business, you were
taught that knowledge about what you do was what
you needed to become successful. That your
"professionalism" will be the key to success. That
people will come to you once they hear about how
"good" you are. You were probably given the age old
"wisdom" that says if you build abetter mousetrap,
the world will beat apath to your door! "Yeah, right!"
Let's speak honestly to each other.
Which brings me to my first, and possibly,
most important point: Knowing how to get
customers, clients or patients is infinitely more
important than any other knowledge you may
attain! I've never known any way to make my
business (and thus, my personal life) better unless I
have aconstant, never-ending flow of prospects and
customers (or clients/patients) who've all called me,
wanting to do business! Iknow that this statement
gets some people upset. That's not my intention. All
I'm trying to do is make the point that until you grasp
the fact that you are not in whatever business you’re
i n . . b u t r a t h e r i n t h e : M A R K E T I N G O F
W H A T E V E R B U S I N E S S Y O U ’ R E I N ! T h a t ' s t h e
bottom line. In fact, no matter what business anyone is
in, they are really not in that business! They are
always in the marketing of that business! And you're
no different. Until you change your psychology to
accept the fact that you are amarketer first, and a
provider of whatever you sell second...you'll never
make the kind of money you want to make! This
acceptance as your role of amarketer is paramount to
changing the size of your bankbook and gaining a
measure of success otherwise unattainable. Let's face
it, in any business...the best marketer wins! Period.
End of story.
And, of course, Iam not implying that you
should just market better and deliver substandard
products or services. No way. Not only must you be
the best marketer, but you must also be the best at
what you do as well. People deserve the best. But, and
this is the big but... it doesn't matter if you're the best
at what you do if you have no one to talk to except
your CO workers and your family! They're nice enough.
I'm sure...but they aren't asubstitute for people who
buy whatever you sell! Getting customers, clients or
patients is the name of the game, and marketing is the
mechanism that gets them! As you read through this
tip sheet and learn the marketing techniques we'll be
discussing...you'll find out that there are two ways to
market: A. Sickeningly wasteful and pushy ways. B.
Inexpensive and response-oriented ways. We'll focus
on type #2, if that's OK with you.
Secret #2: If You Want Your Phone To Ring,
Y o u r A d v e r t i s i n g M u s t G e t P e o p l e ' s
Attentiop...And Arouse Emotions! If you remember
just one secret from this tip sheet, remember to follow
1
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Ultimate Solutions Advertising
this proven five-step formula in every bit of marketing
and advertising you do: 1. Get Attention! 2. Arouse
interest and emotion. 3. Tell an interesting story in a
believable way. 4. Offer an incentive to take
action...NOW. 5. Ask for action, and make it easy for
people to do it.
Spring 2003 Addition
front of the public. You know, the kind of ad that
cleverly includes aho-hum grocery list of services
that anyone in your industry or profession provides.
There is absolutely nothing in the ad that gets
attention or arouses emotions about why someone
should choose YOU versus every other firm listed in
the telephone directory. No curiosity...no
emotions...just very plain-looking ads. Just like
everyone else's. If you want to get people's attention,
you have to be saying things that will actually get
t h e i r a t t e n t i o n !
The first, and most important, step is to get
attention. Your advertising will not stimulate
responses unless it gets read. It will not be read if it is
not noticed. And, it will not be noticed unless it: Gets
People's Attention! In Fact, 80 -90% Of The
Success Of Any Ad Is The Direct Result Of How
Well The Headline Gets...Attention!! If you don’t
believe me, go look at “People Magazine”, “The
Star”, “Redbook” and so on.
Secret #3: Typical Yellow Pages Ads Get Typically
Lousy Results! Another example of boring
advertising is found in any Yellow Pages anywhere in
the country. Most Yellow Pages ads are just abig
blur! They all look exactly the same and do nothing to
get anyone's attention! Can you spot one truly
noticeable and different reason why aprospective
customer or client or patient would be motivated to
pick up the telephone and call one of the companies
listed? The problem? Yellow pages "advertising" like
this is just one big blur of sameness.
There's nothing unique. Everybody looks the same
and says the same stuff. You could literally swap
names and phone numbers on the ads, and no one
would even know the difference! To be asuccessful
advertiser and marketer, you must always remember
that...People Don't Read Ads That Look Like
Advertising and Sales Pitches! The problem here?
People don't really pay attention to ads about you,
your company, or your professional titles. It doesn't
matter how big, how good, how nice or caring you tell
peopleyouare.Mostpeopledon'treadimage^slike
this because they so obviously look like advertising,
and because they do not get anyone's attention. Every
day, your prospects' minds are being flooded with
thousands of advertising messages. Television
c o m m e r c i a l s . R a d i o c o m m e r c i a l s . P o s t e r s a n d
billboards. Newspaper ads. Magazine ads. Mailboxes
filled with sales letters. Advertising on buses, trucks,
streetcars, subways and taxicabs. You even find
advertising pasted on public bathroom walls! There is
no way to stop this daily assault, but we have all
learned to tune out advertising pictures and
messages...unless they get our attention and arouse
our interest. Are you starting to get the idea of what
kind of emotional headlines get responses? The
Secret Is All In The Headlines and Topics You
Warning -What You're About To Read
May Shock You! Whether or not you read...like...or
even approve of ..any of the publications we just
discussed, you'd have to be blind to get past a
magazine rack or grocery checkout line without at
least noticing these covers and attention-getting
headlines. Can you say the same about your
advertising? Are your advertising messages exciting
or boring? What do your business cards, marketing
letters and brochures look like? Are you really saying
or doing anything different from anybody else???
Every month, the editors of all these magazines and
newspapers do everything they can possibly think of
TO GRAB YOUR ATTENTION! While you may
not agree with the pictures or underlying messages,
millions of these magazines are sold each week
because...Emotions stir up more sales than logic
e v e r w i l l !
The biggest mistake you can make in
marketing is to use astraightforweird, "professional"
approach that attempts to appeal to people's
sensibilities. Yet, over 95% of all businesses and
sales people make it over and over! Logical
advertising is dead advertising. Emotions are what
motivate people to take notice and respond. If Your
Advertising Is Boring...It Won't Make Money!
Now stop for amoment and think. Think about your
advertising. Think about your company's advertising.
Think about the brochures you give to people. Are
people beating down your doors and ringing your
phone off the hook to get to you because of what you
do...the titles you hold...or the colorful brochures?

businesses use to get their name and phone number in
